title: Monsoon Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A twist on traditional Moroccan lamb tagine, featuring a tangy and fluffy consistency. This dish is Whole30-friendly and perfect for a rainy monsoon night.

ingredients.name: mixed spice blend
ingredients.notes: including cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and turmeric

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mixed spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the lemon juice, red wine vinegar, diced tomatoes, and chopped parsley.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover the tagine and transfer it to the oven.
Bake at 300°F (150°C) for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Skim any excess fat from the surface of the sauce.
Serve the lamb and sauce over fluffy rice or cauliflower rice.
Garnish with additional parsley and a sprinkle of tadka spice mix, if desired.
